title: vaccinium crassifolium subsp. sempervirens
intro: Vaccinium crassifolium subsp. sempervirens, also known as the evergreen blueberry, is a subspecies of shrub in the heath family that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its berries, which are rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, and have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Vaccinium crassifolium subsp. sempervirens include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its antidiabetic and antimicrobial properties. Vaccinium crassifolium subsp. sempervirens can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and its berries can be eaten fresh or dried and used in jams and preserves.
